title: ## TASK Security review of the telop-pack-srt-02 video generation pipeline, focusing on API key management, TTS localhost binding, GHA Artifacts integrity, and command injection risks. ## EXPECTED OUTCOME Security score (0-3) with vulnerability list and remediations. ## CONTEXT ### Architecture: - T...
summary_text: ```json { "score": 1, "vulnerabilities": [ { "severity": "critical", "type": "Command injection (shell quoting via filename)", "file": ".github/workflows/note-review.yml", "description": "The workflow interpolates a dynamically-generated file list into a multi-line shell script argument: `--files \"${{ needs.detect_changes.outputs.changed_files }}\"`. If a changed manuscript filename contains a double-quote/newline, it can break shell quoting and inject arbitrary commands in the runner context. This is reachable because PRs can introduce oddly-named files under `note_manuscripts/**`.", "remediation": "Stop passing filenames through the shell as a single quoted string. Use a JSON output mode (or write the list to a file artifact) and have Node read/parse it safely. Also reject/escape filenames containing `\\n`, `\\r`, `\"`, and other shell-metacharacters, and consider failing if such filenames are present." }, { "severity": "critical", "type": "Secrets exposure to untrusted code (CI on pull_request)", "file": ".github/workflows/note-review.yml", "description": "On `pull_request`, the workflow checks out and executes repository code (`npm ci` + `npm run ...`) while injecting provider API keys via `env:`. For PRs originating from branches in the same repo (not forks), this allows any contributor who can open a PR to modify code and exfiltrate secrets.", "remediation": "Do not run secret-bearing jobs on `pull_request`. Options: run only on `push` to protected branches; or split into a no-secrets PR check and a separate `workflow_dispatch`/`workflow_run` job that requires manual approval (GitHub Environments); or gate by actor/team allowlist and branch protections; ensure workflow files are CODEOWNERS-protected." }, { "severity": "high", "type": "SSRF / uncontrolled outbound fetch", "file": "bin/run-orchestrator.ts", "description": "The orchestrator extracts URLs from manuscripts and issues `fetch()` HEAD/GET requests to them. This is a classic SSRF surface (OWASP A10:2021) and can be abused for internal network probing or hitting local/link-local endpoints if reachable from the environment where it runs.", "remediation": "Add a strict URL allowlist (scheme=https, known hosts), and block localhost, private ranges, and link-local (e.g., 127.0.0.0/8, 10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16, 169.254/16, ::1, fc00::/7). Add short timeouts, limit concurrency, and consider disabling reference validation in CI by default." }, { "severity": "high", "type": "Supply chain risk (unpinned GitHub Actions)", "file": ".github/workflows/note-review.yml", "description": "Third-party actions are referenced by mutable tags (not commit SHAs), including `tj-actions/changed-files@v45`. This increases blast radius of upstream compromise or tag retargeting (OWASP A08:2021).", "remediation": "Pin all actions to full commit SHAs and use Dependabot to update. Prefer GitHub-maintained actions where possible." }, { "severity": "medium", "type": "Software/data integrity failure (artifacts not verified/signed)", "file": ".github/workflows/note-review.yml", "description": "Artifacts are uploaded and later downloaded/consumed without integrity verification (no SHA-256 manifest, no signature, no fail-closed verification).", "remediation": "Emit a SHA-256 manifest for each uploaded artifact (or a consolidated manifest), upload it alongside artifacts, and verify it before consuming artifacts (fail closed). If you want stronger provenance, use Sigstore/cosign attestations or GitHub artifact attestations where feasible." }, { "severity": "medium", "type": "Configuration override risk", "file": "bin/run-agent.ts", "description": "`dotenv.config({ override: true })` allows a checked-in `.env` (added with `git add -f`) to override CI-provided environment variables, which can redirect network calls (e.g., MCP gateway URL) or otherwise subvert expected configuration.", "remediation": "Do not use `override:true`. In CI, skip dotenv entirely (`if (!process.env.CI) dotenv.config()`), and...
